title: Pirate Summer · Scrapbook
notes:
Human-readable catalog of the lore_tokens Pirate Summer surfaces.  Cross-referenced by SlowstockScrapbook against GauntletState.state.lore_tokens_revealed.  Discovered entries render as title + blurb; undiscovered as silhouettes.

Five tiers:
  · endings (7)         — reached one of the per-camper or shared endings
  · wilson_clues (6)    — discovered a piece of the Wilson mystery
  · cross_lore (5)      — cross-Oneironautics breadcrumbs found
  · world (4)           — world-object discoveries
  · completion (2)      — top-level tokens the run always writes

entries.title: The Pirate ending
entries.blurb: You unlocked the six-clue mystery and Wilson gave you the satchel.  Sam is still understanding what that means, thirty summers later.

entries.title: The Plotter ending
entries.blurb: Sam and Tessa became each other's maids of honor.  Tessa's plans have never once been wrong.  Sam has verified this.

entries.title: The Reader ending
entries.blurb: Sam became a librarian.  Wu Kai's Earthsea paperbacks sit in Sam's living room.  Sam has not opened them yet.

entries.title: The Wanderer ending
entries.blurb: Nika sent postcards from eleven cities.  Sam stays where Nika can find Sam.  This is Sam's version of taking care.

entries.title: The Birder ending
entries.blurb: Sam saw 617 species.  The 617th was a marbled murrelet, from Ecola State Park's deck, with Elias.

entries.title: The Homesick Kid ending
entries.blurb: Sam became a fourth-grade teacher.  Every year there is exactly one Danny Broz in the class.  Sam sits with them.

entries.title: The shared ending
entries.blurb: Sam went home with a duffel full of laundry and a summer inside their head.  The camp burned down in 2018.  Sam did not cry.  Sam does not know why.

entries.title: Left Camp Early
entries.blurb: Sam walked to the bus stop at 3:12 PM Friday.  Something at camp had gotten close enough to matter.  Sam walked away from it before it could complete.

entries.title: Wilson clue 1 · the anchor decal
entries.blurb: The chipped anchor decal on Wilson's water bottle · the first sign he had a life at sea.

entries.title: Wilson clue 2 · the Tuesday absence
entries.blurb: Twenty minutes during Homesick Night, and a wet jacket on return.  Priya later confirmed the salt residue · not lake water.

entries.title: Wilson clue 3 · the coordinates
entries.blurb: The Ghost Pirate Play's coordinates · 44° 45' N, 124° 03' W · are Camp Sweetgum's exact coordinates.  Wilson wrote the script.

entries.title: Wilson clue 4 · the buried map
entries.blurb: The map from the coffee tin under The Old Man · signed W.A. — 1987.  You needed Bea or three party members to lift the log.

entries.title: Wilson clue 5 · the shanty
entries.blurb: 'A Nau Catrineta' · a ship that lost its way for years and came back.  Amelie's grandmother sang it.  Wilson knows every verse.

entries.title: Wilson clue 6 · the direct question
entries.blurb: You stood at the Old Man with the map in your hand.  Wilson came up the trail without being called.  You asked.  He said yes.

entries.title: Northwind Harbor · in Sam's duffel
entries.blurb: Sam brought a slowstick from home.  Same publisher as Pirate Summer · Oneironautics.  Wu Kai's kind of thing.

entries.title: Oneironautics · in Portland
entries.blurb: Wu Kai's cousin's friend's mother works there.  The studio is quiet.  They don't play music while they work.

entries.title: Mrs. Wu's Garden · a flier
entries.blurb: Fall open house in Corvallis, June 15, 1995.  A real Mrs. Wu made a real garden.  There is a real game about her.

entries.title: Andersson built the mess hall
entries.blurb: 1952, alone, three months, one helper for the roof.  His grandson runs a hardware store in Depoe Bay.

entries.title: Amelie's grandmother · Coimbra
entries.blurb: The University of Coimbra has a library from 1717.  She used to walk past it.  She said the walls remember her.

entries.title: Ashford Cade · 2016
entries.blurb: A hunter's note tacked to a cabin door in the east forest.  Dated in a year that hasn't happened yet.  Ashford Cade reappears in Estuary 4.  Wu Kai: 'the paper is not from now.  From later.'

entries.title: The 1873 grave
entries.blurb: A stone marker under the spruces · one of the Ana Faustina's crew, who made it up the beach and no further.  Wilson has kept the moss off the stone twice.

entries.title: The bag has metal in it
entries.blurb: Wu Kai's insight after watching Wilson.  Bea confirmed by brushing against it on Sunday.  Ford noticed the salt water stain on the seam.

entries.title: Sailor's leather shoes
entries.blurb: Ford noticed.  Old design, salt-softened.  Not what a camp counselor buys.  Bea's uncle had shoes like that.

entries.title: Xavier's setup-pause-payoff
entries.blurb: Elias noticed.  Chickadee rhythm.  Xavier doesn't know he's doing bird-call meter.  He probably shouldn't be told.

entries.title: The tide chart · pinned
entries.blurb: Ford's three copies · one for himself, one for Jenny, one for Bear.  Bear appreciated it without having asked for one.

entries.title: You finished Pirate Summer
entries.blurb: One week at Camp Sweetgum in July of 1994.  Whatever you carried out with you was what you carried out with you.

entries.title: You went to Camp Sweetgum
entries.blurb: Fourteen campers, three counselors, one Sitka spruce longer than a Volkswagen bus.  You were there.

entries.title: The Pirate · Absolved
entries.blurb: You brought the captain's word back to Wilson.  Wilson called his father on Sunday morning.  His father said · come home.  Wilson went home in October.

entries.title: Wilson's 1988 logbook
entries.blurb: Nika opened the padlock in fifteen seconds without explaining how.  Or Priya noticed the shackle wear.  Either way, you read his own hand from six years ago.

entries.title: The boathouse padlock, off
entries.blurb: Reggie's dad's third key worked.  Or Nika's fingers did.  Either way, the door opened.

entries.title: First mate, later master
entries.blurb: 1985 to 1988 · Wilson worked a coastal freighter out of Astoria.  Ford's father has photos of that boat somewhere.

entries.title: 'Ready to be who I was'
entries.blurb: Wilson's own words from July 1988, buried under the log with the tin.  Six years is a long time to be getting close to ready.

entries.title: Ana Faustina · 1873
entries.blurb: A Portuguese brig · 89 tons, out of Porto · carrying cochineal dye and a small library.  Wu Kai knew her before you did.

entries.title: PERDIDA · MARÇO 1873
entries.blurb: The March 1873 Pacific low sat over this coast for eleven days.  Seventeen wrecks between the Columbia and Cape Blanco.  She was one of them.

entries.title: The barrel puzzle · verse two
entries.blurb: Wu Kai reads the shanty in order · the rhymes are the release mechanism.  Or three of you force it.  Inside · dry rope, a rusted lantern, a folded oilcloth.

entries.title: 'I made it this far · W.A. 1988'
entries.blurb: Wilson's hand on the cave wall at Sam's eye level.  Six years ago he stood exactly here and turned back for a light.

entries.title: The drowned corridor · through
entries.blurb: Ollie held your hand and led you under · advanced-swim after all.  Or Ford tide-timed the ninety-second window.  You made it through.

entries.title: The satchel
entries.blurb: Silver whistle · folded chart · March 12, 1873 letter.  The treasure was Camp Sweetgum all along · the crew never got here.

entries.title: The captain's letter · March 12
entries.blurb: Portuguese, dated three days before the storm.  Amelie's grandmother can translate.  Amelie asked her when she got home.

entries.title: The chart's center
entries.blurb: Portuguese Mercator notation, cross-hairs at 44° 45' N, 124° 03' W · the Camp Sweetgum bay exactly.  They knew where they were headed.  They just never got here.

entries.title: 'Tell him to come home'
entries.blurb: The Ana Faustina's captain forgave Wilson's great-great-grandfather for surviving.  You carried the message back.  Ford: 'you were on the ship for less than a minute of real time.'

entries.title: 'this one's a good one'
entries.blurb: You told Bea the boulder was a good one on the Wednesday hike.  She sat on it for two minutes.  You waited.

entries.title: The Homesick-Night silence
entries.blurb: You sat with Wu Kai through the song and said nothing.  When it ended he said 'thank you.'  That was enough.

entries.title: 'ask Elias which bird'
entries.blurb: You asked, on the hike.  Elias named it and three others.  You did not, at any point, interrupt.  Elias will remember.

entries.title: The 11:47 shoulder
entries.blurb: Danny fell asleep against your shoulder on the campfire bench.  Marisol showed up and helped carry him back without a word.

entries.title: Nika's spot
entries.blurb: You sat in Nika's seat during the Talent Show.  When she came back at 9:47 PM she sat next to you.  At breakfast · 'thanks for not telling.'

entries.title: BEGINNER swim, publicly
entries.blurb: You said it loud enough for Ollie to hear on Monday.  Ollie sat next to you at lunch.  Ollie did not almost drown on Thursday.  Not a coincidence.

entries.title: Fourteen names on the first pass
entries.blurb: You named them all back at Sunday's name game.  Tessa said 'okay.  huh.'  Which, from Tessa, is roughly a standing ovation.

entries.title: Congratulations, publicly
entries.blurb: You said it loud enough for the whole range to hear.  Marisol left a marshmallow on your pillow that night.  She did not explain.  She did not need to.

entries.title: The man, not the boat
entries.blurb: You asked Reggie about his dad the person.  Reggie said 'when he's home we go on the boat.'  Then Reggie asked about your dad.  You answered.

entries.title: One line, off-key
entries.blurb: You sang along with Sylvie during the Talent Show.  She held the last note six seconds longer so you could catch up.  'You were listening.  That is the whole thing.'

entries.title: 'honest'
entries.blurb: You used exactly that word about her gray shirt.  Amelie: 'my grandmother says that too.  Do you know my grandmother.  You would like each other.'

entries.title: The sulfite label, before
entries.blurb: You caught Priya's hand before it went in the vat.  Priya read the label twice.  Priya's dad heard about you two weeks later at Sunday dinner.
